summary: Add a way to provide a default value when looking up a key in a Document
description:
In many languages (including Swift) there is a way to provide a default value to use for a key that isn't present in a dictionary. 

The Swift-y way incorporates the value into the subscript getter:
{code:swift}let value = doc["x", default: 0]{code}
See documentation [here|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/dictionary/2894528-subscript].

We should consider supporting this on Documents. 

Note we will need to think carefully about behavior when there are null values in documents.

summary: bsonEquals does not work for arrays of different length
description:
This test fails:

```

func testBsonArrays() {
 let b0: [BSONValue] = [1, 2]
 let b1: [BSONValue] = [1, 2, 3]
 XCTAssertFalse(bsonEquals(b0, b1))
 }```

summary: Allow .uuidDecodingStrategy and .dateDecodingStrategy to be set for BSONDecoder
Use generated collection names for tests
description:
As discussed in [mongodb/mongo-swift-driver#97|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-swift-driver/pull/97#discussion_r215792572], we should use generated names for test collections. This would minimize the chance of different tests using the same collection and inadvertently running with pre-existing data. It may also allow us to leave data behind for failed tests if we like (PHPLIB does this).

The database name can be constant (where possible) throughout the test suite.

summary: Allow .uuidEncodingStrategy and .dateEncodingStrategy to be set on BSONEncoder
description: Same principal as SWIFT-281, but for the encoder.

summary: bsonEquals should be public
description:
Implementing SWIFT-217, I caught that {{bsonEquals}} does not have an access level specified and it is therefore {{internal}}. 

I've corrected it in that PR to appease the linter, but I'm opening a separate ticket to ensure that we get this change in for the 0.0.8 release, regardless of where that PR is when we tag the release.

While the newly added {{explicit_acl}} rule will probably catch most of these cases, I believe this further supports the suggestion made in SWIFT-292 that we move toward separately testing private and public API.

summary: BSONNull should be Equatable
description: The rest of our public BSONValue types are, so we should be consistent.

summary: retryWrites is not set on client
description:
Currently, we do not set the retryWrites option in the {{MongoClient}} initializer, regardless of whether the user specifies the option or not.

Currently there is no way to set it directly on the client in libmongoc, so we will have to set it on the URI before creating the {{mongoc_client_t}} .

summary: Unify decoding and encoding strategies
description:
Right now, the encoding strategies are defined as {{.deferTo}} ,whereas the decoding strategies are defined as {{.deferredTo}}. We should refactor the encoding strategies to be in line with both the decoding ones and with {{JSONEncoder}} .

Also, the documentation should be changed to use similar wording.

summary: MongoMobile should cleanup only when instructed
description: Currently, MongoMobile cleans up every time [{{MongoMobile.close()}}|https://github.com/mongodb/swift-mongo-mobile/blob/b0893b682a5305bceb51305d6b11818f2c1aa5f6/Sources/MongoMobile/MongoMobile.swift#L105] is called. This means you cannot close and reopen an instance, which effectively means that you can only use one db path per session. This is not expected behaviour.

summary: AnyBSONValue should conform to Hashable
description:
{{BSONValue}} does not currently conform to {{Hashable}}, but it should, for the same reason it should conform to {{Equatable}}, since all {{BSONValue}}'s are themselves {{Hashable}}.

[~jason.flax] provided a sketch for what the implementation of this may look like:
{code:java}
private func _hash(into hasher: inout Hasher, bsonValue: BSONValue?) {
    switch (bsonValue) {
    case (let value as Int):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Int32):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Int64):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Double):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as Decimal128):
        hasher.combine(value.data)
    case (let value as Bool):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as String):
        hasher.combine(value)
    case (let value as RegularExpression):
        hasher.combine(value.options)
        hasher.combine(value.pattern)
    case (let value as Timestamp):
        hasher.combine(value.timestamp)
    case (let value as Date):
        hasher.combine(value.timeIntervalSince1970)
    case (_ as MinKey):
        hasher.combine(1)
    case (_ as MaxKey):
        hasher.combine(1)
    case (let value as ObjectId):
        hasher.combine(value.description)
    case (let value as CodeWithScope):
        hasher.combine(value.code)
        _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: value.scope)
    case (let value as Binary):
        hasher.combine(value.data)
    case (let value as Document):
        hasher.combine(value.canonicalExtendedJSON)
    case (let value as [BSONValue?]): // TODO: SWIFT-242
        return value.forEach { _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: $0!) }
    default: break
    }
}

extension BSONValue {
    func hash(into hasher: inout Hasher) {
        _hash(into: &hasher, bsonValue: self)
    }
}
{code}

It is worth noting that unlike SWIFT-254, this does (should) _not_ depend on SWIFT-215 to be implemented.

summary: When a document is missing an `_id`, prepend one to a copy of the document before sending to server
description: To be in line with other drivers we should prepend the _id field before sending to the server.

summary: Do not modify input documents missing _ids in insertOne and insertMany
Add a .dataEncodingStrategy and .dataDecodingStrategy to support storing Data in BSON binary format
description:
The {{encode(to: encoder Encoder)}} implementation for {{Data}} writes a {{[UInt8]}} to the provided container. The {{init(from decoder: Decoder)}} implementation attempts to decode a {{[UInt8]}} from a container. 

Since {{BSON}} does not support integer types besides {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, we ended up writing logic (see the {{CodableNumber}} protocol) that will store {{UInt8s}} as {{Int32}} s in a document, and that will allow decoding any {{Int32}} or {{Int64}} that can fit into a {{UInt8}} as a {{UInt8}}.

All of that is to say that round-tripping a struct containing {{Data}} with the encoder and decoder works, but it is inefficient, as each byte ends up taking up 4 bytes in the document!

Our {{Binary}} type is initializable from {{Data}}, so for optimal encoding users should wrap their {{Data}} in a {{Binary}} before its encoded, and then request a {{Binary}} from the decoder.

However, users do not always have control over the types they are using, so it would be nice to allow setting a {{.dataEncodingStrategy}} of {{.binary}} that automatically wraps the {{Data}} in a {{Binary}}. Similarly, {{.dataDecodingStrategy = .binary}} would look for a {{Binary}} in the document being decoded from.

summary: Consider whether to be using fatalErrors vs precondition/preconditionFailures
description:
[precondition|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1540960-precondition] and [preconditionFailure|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1539374-preconditionfailure] are not evaluated in unchecked builds ( -Ouncheck), but [fatalError|https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/1538698-fatalerror]s are.

we pretty much just use precondition/preconditionFailure everywhere. Swift std lib uses a mix of both.

we should further research this and come up with a rule of thumb as to whether to use fatalError or precondition in various places, and correct any existing violations. 

blog posts that seem promising for learning more:
 * [https://agostini.tech/2017/10/01/assert-precondition-and-fatal-error-in-swift/]
 * [https://blog.krzyzanowskim.com/2015/03/09/swift-asserts-the-missing-manual/] 
 * [https://www.swiftbysundell.com/posts/picking-the-right-way-of-failing-in-swift]

summary: Introduce new errors and error types
description:
Add definitions for the new error enums and error details structs.

The errors are as follows:

*ServerError*
 commandError(code, message)
 * Thrown when commands that experience errors on the server side that prevent execution
 * From the Write Commands spec: “a command failure, which indicates the command itself did not complete successfully. Example command failures include failure to authorize, failure to parse, operation aborted by user, and unexpected errors during execution (these should be very rare).”

writeError(write: WriteErrorDetails?, wc: WriteConcernErrorDetails?)
 * Thrown from write commands that experience errors on the server side
 
bulkWriteError(writeErrors: [BulkWriteErrorDetails]?, writeConcernError: WriteConcernErrorDetails?, result: BulkWriteResult)
 * Thrown from Bulk write operations that experience errors on the server side

*UserError*
 logicError(message)
 * Thrown when user uses the driver in an improper way

invalidArgument(message)
 * Thrown when receiving an error with code COMMAND_INVALID_ARG from a mongoc call or when we can determine invalid arguments at the Swift level

*RuntimeError*
 internalError(message)
 * Thrown when something is null when it shouldn’t be, mongoc has an internal error, can’t understand server response
 * Should generally be indicative of a bug, either in Swift, c driver, or mongod

connectionError(message)
 * Any connection related failures (MONGOC_ERROR_STREAM domain)

authenticationError(message)
 * Any auth related failures (MONGOC_ERROR_CLIENT_AUTHENTICATE)

Also, define a function that will return an appropriate error given a libmongoc error code and domain.

summary: Update CRUD API errors to match the new hierarchy
description:
We don't really follow the CRUD API's specification of error types and all the values that should be included. we should audit these errors and make sure we include the necessary info.

we should also think about ways we can incorporate our BsonDecoder to convert reply documents into errors.

Using the new error hierarchy, we should:
* Throw command errors in the event of actual commandErrors
* Throw appropriate [Bulk]WriteErrors for write errors, complete with results and all errors contained therein
* Remove InsertManyError, throw a BulkWriteError instead.

summary: Convert InsertManyResult to BulkWriteResult when insertMany throws BulkWriteError
description: InsertMany is implemented as a bulk write and is meant to throw a BulkWriteError as per the crud spec. We'll need to convert the InsertManyResult to a BulkWriteResult so it can be included in a BulkWriteError thrown by {{insertMany()}}.

summary: Convert invalidCollection, invalidClient, invalidUri, and invalidResponse errors to internalError or fatalError
description: These "invalidX" errors typically catch when libmongoc returns null (memory allocation failure) or some other internal error occurs. If one of these errors is thrown where it can be recovered from nicely, thrown an {{internalError}}. Otherwise, use a {{fatalError}} .

summary: Convert invalidCursor errors to commandErrors, logicErrors and invalidArgument errors
description:
{{invalidCursor}} is currently used as a catch-all for errors related to cursors. This includes errors in the creation of the cursor and errors that occur in calls to getMore. 

We should distinguish between these by appropriately throwing either {{commandErrors}}, {{logicErrors}}, or {{invalidArgument}} errors.

summary: Convert bsonParseError to internalError
description:
Currently, everywhere bsonParseError is thrown, an internal problem has occurred within libmongoc (e.g. memory allocation failures)

Replace all instances where this is thrown with the new {{internalError}}.

summary: Convert readPreferenceError to invalid argument or logic errors
description:
Currently we throw readPreferenceErrors whenever something related to readPreferences has a problem. We should be more specific and instead throw errors that detail immediately what is wrong.

e.g.
an out of range maxStalenessSeconds is an invalidArgumentError
setting tagSets when the mode is primary is a logicError

summary: Convert bsonEncodeError, bsonDecodeError, typeError to new error types
description:
In our encoders and decoders, we currently throw many different types of errors, some of which are redundant or incorrect.

We should update them to throw encodingError, decodingError, logicErrors, internalErrors, or invalidArgumentErrors as necessary.

Also, when encoding/decoding directly to/from a document without the use of {{BSONEncoder}} / {{BSONDecoder}} (i.e. through {{BSONValue}} conformance methods ), we should be throwing appropriate errors. In particular, we should be throwing {{.logicError}} s when the user attempts to retrieve a value from a {{DocumentIterator}} that doesn't match the current type of the iterator.

summary: Remove old errors
description:
At this point, (almost) all of the old errors should be unused. Remove the definition of {{MongoError}} and cleanup any references to the remaining ones in code.

File follow up tickets as necessary for any lingering issues.

summary: Move RegularExpression.nsRegularExpression logic into a new NSRegularExpression initializer
description:
Because {{NSRegularExpression}} doesn't quite align with a BSON regular expression, we chose to write our own {{RegularExpression}} type and provide facilities on it for converting to an from {{NSRegularExpression}} (with some lossiness of options). 

The {{NSRegularExpression}} -> {{RegularExpression}} conversion is implemented via an initializer: {{RegularExpression.init(from regex: NSRegularExpression)}}. 

The opposite conversion is implemented as a computed property on {{RegularExpression}}: {{var nsRegularExpression: NSRegularExpression}}. 

I think it would be more consistent/symmetric to instead add a new convenience initializer to {{NSRegularExpression}} via extension that takes in a {{RegularExpression}}.

summary: Don't require connectionString label in MongoClient initializer
Correctly encode strings with multi-byte UTF8 characters
description:
All C driver methods that take in strings also take in an Int32 corresponding to the string's length.
In all these cases, we use the {{String.count}} property to get the length.
However, to handle multi-byte UTF8 characters, we should actually be using {{String.utf8.count}}.

Related reading: 
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/string
https://developer.apple.com/documentation/swift/string/utf8view

summary: Allow a user logger to be set in MongoMobile
description: This should use mongo_embedded_v1_log_callback: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo/blob/master/src/mongo/embedded/mongo_embedded/mongo_embedded.h#L366

summary: Throw an error when a non-BSONEncoder is used in encode(to:) for all BSONValue types that we own
description: For BSONValue types that we created (not Swift types we extend), we should throw errors when you try to encode them using a non-BSON encoder, as the synthesized implementations are often incorrect.

summary: Throw an error when non-BSONDecoder is used in init(from: decoder) for BSONValue types
description: For BSONValue types that we created (not Swift types we extend), we should throw errors when you try to decode them using a non-BSON decoder, as the synthesized implementations are often incorrect.

summary: Improve error reporting in mobile library
description:
Before we do a 1.0 release of swift-mongo-mobile, we should improve our error reporting.

Things to consider:
 * We have a couple of {{MongoMobileError}} cases that are never used.
 * Another couple cases have empty closures as associated values, ie they are defined as something like {{invalidClient()}}. It is unnecessary to do that. These cases should just be defined as {{invalidClient}} with no parentheses. 
 * In cases where the embedded library was already initialized or cleaned up (we can tell this by whether the shared {{libraryInstance}} property is non-nil), we could short-circuit and provide a more specific error, like {{MongoMobileError.logicError}} or something like that. This would be consistent with the driver and allow distinguishing between user error (i.e. repeated calling {{initialize}} and {{close}}) and any other cases that can cause the embedded lib init and cleanup methods to fail. 
 * We have two error types, {{MongoMobileError}} and {{MongoEmbeddedV1Error}}. Arguably, the second could just be a case of the first, rather than its own type. 

We should also clearly document what types of errors each method can throw.

summary: Investigate XCode 10.2 Issues
Round trip Symbols, DBPointers, and Undefineds correctly
description:
When we encounter the deprecated BSON types Symbol and DBPointer in documents, we return them as Strings and Documents, respectively. So if you round trip these types you do not get the same result out. 

When we encounter a BSON undefined, we just error, which is even worse. This has been a problem for the Stitch team as they decode server responses that may contain an undefined.

We should expose all of these types in the public API, but make their initializers internal so that users cannot create new ones themselves in the driver, and can only read them out of existing documents.

summary: Test round-tripping of BSON types with native representations using the BSON corpus data
description:
The BSON corpus test runner is written to follow the specification instructions for a language with no native intermediate representation of BSON. While it is true we do not have a native representation of a document, since we just wrap {{bson_t}} s which are already stored in BSON format, we *do* have native representations of all the other BSON types.

We should implement the round trip tests for languages with native representations by adding assertions that do something like: 
 1. Parse a document from the raw data
 2. Recursively convert the document to a {{[String: BSONValue]}}. This will convert every stored value to a native Swift type. (I don't think we can use the decoder here, as it will not recursively parse nested documents into maps.)
 3. Recursively convert the {{[String: BSONValue]}} back into a document. This will encode every stored value back to BSON. 
 4. Check that the new document matches the input

(Note: may need to do something special to preserve key order since dictionaries don't guarantee it)

This would allow us to catch bugs like SWIFT-351 for example – there are [BSON corpus cases for multi-byte UTF8|https://github.com/mongodb/specifications/blob/8d1187be4094418ed130e740c067b8de20b65d92/source/bson-corpus/tests/string.json#L22], and converting the stored strings to Swift strings and then putting them back into documents would have shown us that we cut off the end of string.

summary: Improve error message for type mismatches when decoding driver introduced BSON types
Make bson pointer access explicit
description: We pass references to {{bson_t}} and {{bson_iter_t}} liberally in the codebase, leading to some confusion about whether the pointers are being used in a mutable way or not. Additionally, by making this more explicit we can create easy paths for supporting a change to Swift 5 where these types are imported as {{OpaquePointer}}

summary: Include bsonEquals as part of BSONValue protocol
Provide an internal accessor for `bson_t`s length
description: In Swift 5 {{bson_t}} is imported as an {{OpaquePointer}}, meaning that we will lose type information from within Swift. As a result, we no longer have a way to determine the length of the {{bson_t}}. We can provide a method in our {{swift-bson}} package to provide this functionality.

summary: TopologyDescription initializer doesn't clean up server descriptions array
description: docs for [mongoc_topology_description_get_servers|http://mongoc.org/libmongoc/current/mongoc_topology_description_get_servers.html] say we need to call  {{mongoc_server_descriptions_destroy_all}}, but we don't.

summary: Ensure all bson_ts are correctly cleaned up
description:
An issue reported on GitHub (linked below) led us to discover a few different places in the driver where we call `bson_new_from_x` (x being data or json), and then pass the result into {{Document(fromPointer)}} (which does a {{bson_copy}}), but never clean up the result. 
We should ensure that every time we call these methods we clean up their results properly.

We can either just add {{bson_destroy}} s, or a more performant option would be to add a new {{DocumentStorage}} initializer that "steals" the {{bson_t}} rather than copying its memory.

summary: Provide better consistency around Int usage with Documents
description:
the {{Int}} type in Swift is tricky to handle correctly, because its bitwidth is machine-dependent. But it is also used far more often than {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, so it's important that the behavior is intuitive to users.

The current behavior is as follows:

Putting into documents:
 * If the {{Int}} will fit in an {{Int32}}, its {{bsonType}} is considered {{.int32}} and it will be encoded to the document as an {{Int32}}. 
 * Otherwise, its {{bsonType}} is {{.int64}} and it will be encoded to the document as an {{Int64}}.

Reading out of documents:
 * Any value encoded as an {{Int32}} will be returned as an {{Int}} 
 * Any value encoded as an {{Int64}} will be returned as an {{Int64}} 

There are some problems with this behavior. As [~jmikola] pointed out: consider a user who stores an {{Int}}, which could be anything in {{[Int.min, Int.max]}}. 

 
{code:java}let a = Int(Int64.max)
let doc: Document = ["a": a]
print(a) // 9223372036854775807
print(doc["a"] as? Int) // nil
print(doc["a"] as? Int64) // Optional(9223372036854775807)

let b = Int(Int32.max)
let doc: Document = ["b": b] 
print(b) // 2147483647 
print(doc["b"] as? Int) // Optional(2147483647)
print(doc["b"] as? Int64) // nil{code}
When the user retrieves the value for that key, they will get back a {{BsonValue?}}. If the user then wants to cast the value to a type, they must either know ahead of time how big the value is, or alternatively switch on the {{bsonType}} property, or just try both {{Int}} and {{Int64}} and see which works.

It's not clear what the best solution is, but we should aim for something with consistent/predictable behavior that does not require the user having prior knowledge of the value stored. 

The way MongoKitten handles this is by always encoding {{Int}} as {{Int64}}.

Inspired by that approach, one possibility is:
 * Always convert an {{Int}} to an {{Int64}} and then write it to the doc.
 * When reading an integer value from a doc, return {{Int32}} or {{Int64}}. whatever it was encoded as
 * Upsides:
 ** predictable behavior. {{Int32}} and {{Int64}} preserve their types when round-tripped, and {{Int}} always becomes {{Int64}}.
 * Downsides:
 ** on a 64-bit platform, half of the {{Int}} range can be represented with just 32 bits. on a 32-bit platform, the entire {{Int}} range can be represented with just 32 bits. so this isn't particularly efficient in those cases space-wise. 
 ** {{Int}} is used a lot more commonly throughout Swift libraries than {{Int32}} and {{Int64}}, so users are going to have to do a lot of casting.

summary: Store bson_oid_t in ObjectId rather than creating one each time we need it
description:
Currently, ObjectId stores its data as a String. When we need to interact with libmongoc, we use this to initialize a bson_oid_t. 

this is rather costly and there is no clear reason why we aren't just keeping around and reusing one bson_oid_t the whole time.

we should do that instead, and expose the String version of the ObjectID, along with the timestamp, as computed properties.

summary: Enable evergreen builds on macos
description: {{swiftenv}} currently requires sudo privileges, and wants to install versions of swift to the root ({{ / }}) directory, which we are not allowed to do on our evergreen macos hosts. A patch to {{swiftenv}} could allow for this, but until then we will have to keep testing our builds on travis.

summary: Modify Vapor example to show using one client per eventloop
description: Since {{MongoCollection}} is not thread safe, we should edit the example so its clear to users they need to use a different client in each thread.

summary: expireAfter index option is not correctly encoded
Document.count should not be publicly settable
description: We should mark it {{internal(set)}} so that users can't update it themselves.

summary: Resync retryable writes tests
description: See DRIVERS-476 for details.

summary: Initial aggregate command for a change stream should not be retried
description: See DRIVERS-474 for details.

summary: Update ChangeStream spec to detail usage of startAtOperationTime
description: See DRIVERS-506 for details.

summary: Remove cursor `snapshot` option
description: See DRIVERS-454 for details.
